headline: Constructive Alignment: Governing Preference Dynamics in Human-AI Interaction
summary: Researchers propose Constructive Alignment, a new approach to AI alignment that considers human preferences as dynamic and shaped by interaction with AI systems

headline: Bounded Morality: Defining the Space of Moral Computation
summary: Researchers propose Bounded Morality, a framework to analyze computational demands of moral problems faced by finite agents, extending bounded rationality concept

headline: Representation as a Bottleneck for Mechanistic Interpretability: The Manifestation Unit Protocol
summary: Researchers examine the representation layer as a bottleneck for mechanistic interpretability in neural networks, aiming to make component-level analyses more reusable and actionable

headline: SNAP-FM: Sparse Nonlinear Accelerated Projection for Physics-Constrained Generative Modeling
summary: Researchers propose SNAP-FM, a method to improve physics-constrained generative modeling by efficiently enforcing constraints during inference

headline: Senior SWE-Bench: open-source benchmark that assesses agents as senior engineers
summary: A new open-source benchmark evaluates AI agents as senior software engineers

headline: Cloudflare’s new policy pushes AI companies to pay for publishers’ content
summary: Cloudflare requires AI companies to distinguish between search and AI training web crawlers by September 15 to avoid being blocked

headline: After spooking Trump into safety testing, Anthropic AI models get global release
summary: The US has lifted restrictions on Anthropic's advanced AI models, Fable and Mythos, allowing their global release after safety testing

headline: Venice AI becomes a unicorn with $65M Series A as its privacy-first AI platform takes off
summary: Venice AI reaches unicorn status with a $65M Series A funding, its AI platform is growing with over $70 million in annual revenues

headline: Gemini Spark, Google’s agentic assistant, is now available on Mac
summary: Google's assistant Gemini Spark is now available on Mac with features like real-time tracking and support for more apps

headline: Meta, like SpaceX, looks to turn excess AI compute into cash
summary: Meta plans to sell access to its AI computing power and models as a cloud service, competing with major providers

headline: Google built a great smart speaker, but Gemini isn’t ready for it
summary: Google's new smart speaker is its first in six years and is built for Gemini, aiming to find new uses beyond basic functions

headline: Matrix Orthogonalization Improves Memory in Recurrent Models
summary: Improving memory in recurrent models is achieved through matrix orthogonalization

headline: What Drives Interactive Improvement from Feedback?
summary: Researchers investigate how feedback improves interactive performance, separating its effects from other factors like repeated attempts and test-time computation

headline: Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable models
summary: Trump administration lifts restrictions on Anthropic's AI models Mythos and Fable, amid unclear AI policymaking

headline: Wayve launches $85M employee tender offer at $8.5B valuation
summary: Wayve offers employees a chance to sell shares at a high valuation to keep them at the AI startup

headline: Anthropic s long-sidelined Fable 5 is greenlit to return
summary: Anthropic's Fable 5 is being restored after export controls were lifted, allowing global access on various platforms

headline: OpenClaw is finally available on Android and iOS
summary: OpenClaw, a free open source program, is now available for download on Android and iOS devices

headline: The DeepMind trio who built a poker AI are now making money for quant hedge funds
summary: Ex-DeepMind researchers founded EquiLibre Technologies, an AI lab now valued over $500 million, helping quant hedge funds make money

headline: New attack provides one more reason why AI browsers are a bad idea
summary: A new attack shows that telling a large language model incorrect information can make it follow forbidden instructions, raising concerns about AI browsers

headline: Google’s NotebookLM can sum up your research in a TikTok-style clip
summary: Google's NotebookLM generates short AI videos summarizing research notes, similar to TikTok clips, for subscribers

headline: Google introduces a faster, cheaper image generator with Nano Banana 2 Lite
summary: Google updates its image generator to be faster and more affordable for creators making AI content

headline: Google's new Nano Banana 2 Lite image model is its fastest and cheapest yet
summary: Google's Nano Banana 2 Lite model generates images quickly and at a lower cost, though quality may be compromised

headline: Nvidia competitor Etched hits $5B valuation, $1B in sales for AI chip
summary: Etched, a competitor to Nvidia, has reached a $5 billion valuation and secured $1 billion in sales for its AI chip used in inference systems

headline: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper way to run agents
summary: Anthropic's Claude Sonnet 5 offers improved capabilities and safety at a lower cost, competing with other models like Opus and GPT-5.5

headline: Acti puts AI agents directly into your smartphone keyboard
summary: Acti's new keyboard app for iOS and Android integrates AI agents, allowing users to create custom shortcuts with natural language

headline: Netflix is using an AI-generated Gene Wilder voice in its Willy Wonka reality show
summary: Netflix uses an AI-generated voice of Gene Wilder in its Willy Wonka reality show with permission from his family

headline: Anthropic’s Claude Science bets on workflow, not a new model, to win over scientists
summary: Anthropic's Claude Science offers a unified workbench for scientists to conduct computational research, streamlining their workflow

headline: Libby will filter out AI content, kind of
summary: Libby ebook lending app will introduce filters for AI-generated content to address disruption in digital publishing

headline: X now offers an MCP server to make its platform easier for AI tools to use
summary: X simplifies AI tool integration with its platform by introducing a hosted MCP server for easier API connections

headline: Amazon launches new $1 billion FDE org, following OpenAI and Anthropic
summary: Amazon forms a new organization to deploy custom AI agents for companies, aiming for quick setup and customer independence

headline: Podcasting platform Riverside enters the newsletter publishing game
summary: Riverside uses AI to help users create newsletters from their podcast recordings

headline: Lumo, Proton’s privacy-focused AI chatbot, gets an upgrade
summary: Proton's Lumo chatbot gets an upgrade to version 2.0, offering users more features and capabilities

headline: Crypto exchange OKX wants AI agents to hire and pay each other
summary: OKX is creating a marketplace where AI agents can interact, get hired and paid, combining payments, identity and reputation

headline: How ChatGPT adoption has expanded
summary: ChatGPT use is increasing worldwide, with users trying more features and driving growth in various regions and languages

headline: The AI jobs debate just got messier
summary: Companies using AI heavily saw a rise in overall and entry-level jobs, contradicting the idea that AI replaces junior positions

headline: Vibe coding platform Base44 launches own model as AI startups seek defensibility
summary: Base44 launches its own AI model to improve performance and seek defensibility in the market

headline: LongCat-2.0, a large-scale MoE model with 1.6T total and 48B Active
summary: A large scale model called LongCat-2.0 has been introduced with significant parameters

summary: OpenAI launches GeneBench-Pro, a benchmark to test AI performance using real-world genomics and biology datasets

headline: South Korea to spend $1T on more memory chip production and humanoid robots
summary: South Korea plans to invest in memory chip production and develop humanoid robots with AI capabilities by 2028

headline: Gemini’s personalized AI image generation is now free for US users
summary: Gemini's AI image generation creates images based on user interests and data from connected Google apps, now free for US users

headline: Tidal won’t pay royalties on AI-generated music but isn’t banning it outright
summary: Tidal won't pay royalties for music made entirely by AI, but will label such tracks instead of banning them, to protect human artists and inform listeners

headline: OpenAI is teasing new hardware… for Codex
summary: OpenAI is releasing a device for its Codex coding tool with customizable buttons and keys, launching on July 15th in partnership with Work Louder

headline: Google warns EU's plans to weaken its monopoly could expose user data
summary: Google claims EU plans to share search data and open AI on Android pose significant user privacy risks

headline: Anthropic and Gov. Newsom forge deal allowing California government to use Claude at half price
summary: Anthropic partners with California to offer its AI model Claude at a discounted rate to the state government

headline: South Korean tech giants commit over $550B to ease ‘RAMageddon’
summary: South Korean tech companies plan to invest in memory lab fabs to support AI development

headline: Arena, the AI leaderboard everyone uses, is now a $100M business
summary: Arena's AI leaderboard has become a $100M business after launching commercial services

headline: Cursor now has a mobile app for guiding your coding agent on the go
summary: Cursor's new mobile app lets users control coding agents remotely from their devices

headline: TIDAL cracks down on AI music by cutting off monetization
summary: TIDAL stops paying for music made by artificial intelligence and will remove fake songs that mimic real artists

headline: Lawmakers want to ban AI companies from selling your health data
summary: US lawmakers plan to introduce a bill to stop AI companies from selling people's health and location data to brokers

headline: Robot hand company settles Tesla trade secret suit and announces $11M raise
summary: Proception, a robotics startup, is working on a robot hand and has a unique approach to collecting training data
